one_line_takeaway: AttnDiff fingerprints LLMs via SVD-compressed differential attention responses to single-word-pivot semantic-conflict prompt pairs, compared with CKA, and empirically retains >0.98 similarity for genuine derivatives (SFT, merging, pruning) while staying <0.22-0.23 for unrelated architectures, outperforming parameter- and logit-based baselines that collapse under heavy fine-tuning or structured pruning -- though it requires white-box access and its robustness lacks a formal (non-empirical) guarantee against adaptive, probe-aware evasion.
key_finding: On SFT robustness (Table 1, similarity vs Llama-2-7B), AttnDiff stays at 0.9856-0.9989 (avg 0.9945) across all six suspects including the most heavily adapted ones (CodeLLaMA-7b at 500B tokens: 0.9890; Llemma-7b at 700B tokens: 0.9856), comparable to the strongest baseline REEF (avg 0.9966), while PCS/ICS collapse to ~0.50-0.53 and Logits drops to 0.6367-0.7833 on those two heavily-tuned models; ProFlingo averages only 0.30. On model merging (Table 2), AttnDiff attains >=0.95 similarity to all parents in weight-space merges (Evollm-jp-7b vs its three parents: 0.9726/0.9561/0.9996) and 0.7953/0.7851 on the heterogeneous distribution-level merge (Fusellm-7b vs Openllama-2-7b/Mpt-7b), whereas PCS and Logits score near-zero (0.0000-0.0194/0.0100-0.0000) on those same heterogeneous-merge sources. On pruning (Table 3), AttnDiff never drops below 0.9879 across six structured-pruning (Sheared-LLaMA 1.3B/2.7B variants) and three unstructured-pruning (Sparse/Wanda/GBLM-Llama-2-7b) suspects, while PCS/ICS collapse to 0.0000 under structured pruning of Sheared-LLaMA models. The ablation (Table 4) shows the differential mechanism is what recovers signal under heavy domain adaptation: the non-differential 'Origin' variant drops to 0.5134 (CodeLlama-7b) and 0.2902 (Llemma-7b) vs Llama-2-7B, while the full differential design recovers 0.9890/0.9856 (delta > +0.47/+0.69); for unrelated models the differential design also reduces spurious similarity, e.g., Llama3-8B falls from 0.4751 (Origin) to 0.2299 (Diff), a swing of -0.2452. Probe-count ablation (Fig. 2) shows M<=15 probes yields high spurious cross-family similarity, with M=60 identified as the favorable efficiency/reliability trade-off; the paper's headline separation claim is '>0.98 vs <0.22 with M=60 probes' for related vs. unrelated models.
core_contribution: AttnDiff is a white-box, non-invasive (post-hoc) LLM fingerprinting method for model-provenance/IP verification. It probes a model with pairs of prompts (origin p, corrupted p-tilde) that differ by a single-word lexical pivot flipping the logical entailment (e.g., 'parallel lines never intersect' vs 'always intersect'); extracts per-layer/per-head causal self-attention matrices for both prompts; aligns them to a common token resolution N* via 2D adaptive average pooling when token counts differ; computes a differential attention matrix ΔA = pooled(A-tilde) - pooled(A); compresses ΔA per layer/head with truncated SVD, keeping the top-K singular values as a spectral descriptor; concatenates across layers/heads into a per-probe vector, and stacks over M probes into a fingerprint matrix F ∈ R^(M×LHK). Two models are compared by computing centered linear CKA between the M×M Gram matrices FF^T and F'F'^T, which sidesteps the need for matching feature dimensionality (and hence layer/head count) across architecturally altered suspects. The paper positions this as distinct from four existing intrinsic-fingerprint families (parameter-based, representation-based, adversarial-trigger-based, semantic/output-based), arguing those are respectively fragile to structured pruning, PO/distillation drift, input-perturbation neutralization, and paraphrasing.
threat_model: Post-hoc model forensics: a defender (legitimate model owner) with white-box access to both the original reference model and a suspect model verifies whether the suspect derives from the reference, in a legal/auditing context. The attacker has already obtained the model weights (via theft/unauthorized redistribution) and has full white-box access to apply arbitrary transformations -- SFT, alignment/PO (RLHF-PPO or DPO), pruning, quantization, distillation to a different architecture, and model merging/weight interpolation -- singly or composed, in order to launder provenance while preserving utility. The paper explicitly does not target invasive watermark/backdoor fingerprinting (which requires training-time embedding into a specific victim model) nor strictly black-box API-only settings, since AttnDiff requires access to internal attention activations.
limitations: The paper's own Limitations section (Sec. 8) states: (1) AttnDiff requires white-box access to internal attention activations and does not apply to strictly black-box APIs where only outputs are observable; (2) although Appendix A gives theoretical invariance/stability properties of centered linear CKA and discusses stability of the extraction procedure under model perturbations, the paper lacks a principled end-to-end mathematical model that propagates specific laundering operations (fine-tuning, pruning, merging, distillation) as parameter/architecture transformations through to quantitative bounds on the resulting similarity scores -- this is left as future work. The Discussion section also notes the paper analyzes (in Appendix C, not fully captured in this extraction) a 'probe-aware suppression attack' and a 'probe-refresh mitigation,' implying the fingerprint is not attack-proof against an adaptive adversary who specifically targets the known probe set, and that the proposed defense is to keep probes refreshable/secret rather than the fingerprint being inherently unbreakable. Domain-wise analysis (Table 5) shows expert/specialized models (e.g., CodeLlama) exhibit localized dips in matching domains, and policy-gated 'Safe Alignment' probes show larger deviations under extensive post-training, meaning per-domain scores are less stable than the aggregated global score.

datasets_used: Self-constructed probe set: M=60 origin/corrupted prompt pairs across 6 domains (Code, Math, Economics, Medicine, Daily QA, Safe Alignment), 10 prompts/domain, built via single-word lexical-pivot substitution
Suspect/victim models drawn from Llama-2/3, Qwen2.5 (1.5B-14B), Gemma, and Mistral families and their public derivatives (full list in paper's Appendix F)
Publicly released fine-tuned checkpoints: Llama-2-finance-7b, Vicuna-1.5-7b, WizardMath-7b, Chinese-LLaMA-2-7b, CodeLLaMA-7b, Llemma-7b
Publicly released merged models: Evollm-jp-7b (weight-space merge of Shisa-gamma-7b-v1, Wizardmath-7b-1.1, Abel-7b-002) and Fusellm-7b (distribution-level merge of Llama-2-7b, Openllama-2-7b, Mpt-7b)
Pruned checkpoints: Sheared-LLaMA-1.3B/2.7B variants (structured), Sparse-/Wanda-/GBLM-Llama-2-7b (unstructured), plus additional suspects generated via the LLM-Pruner toolkit
quantitative_results: SFT (Table 1): AttnDiff avg similarity 0.9945 across 6 Llama-2-7B-derived suspects (range 0.9856-0.9989); REEF avg 0.9966; PCS avg 0.8279 (drops to ~0.50-0.53 on CodeLLaMA-7b/Llemma-7b); ICS avg 0.7902; Logits avg 0.8538 (drops to 0.7833/0.6367 on the two heavily-tuned suspects); ProFlingo avg 0.3000; LLMMap avg 0.8541
Model merging weight-space (Table 2, Evollm-jp-7b vs 3 parents): AttnDiff 0.9726/0.9561/0.9996; PCS 0.9992/0.9990/0.9989; REEF 0.9635/0.9526/0.9374

Model merging distribution-level (Table 2, Fusellm-7b vs Llama-2-7b/Openllama-2-7b/Mpt-7b): AttnDiff 0.9962/0.7953/0.7851; PCS 0.9997/0.0194/0.0000; Logits 0.9999/0.0100/0.0000; REEF 0.9996/0.6713/0.6200

Pruning (Table 3): AttnDiff never below 0.9879 across 9 structured/unstructured pruned suspects; PCS collapses to 0.0000 on all 6 structured-pruning Sheared-LLaMA suspects; ICS ranges 0.3510-0.6055 under structured pruning

Ablation of differential mechanism (Table 4): 'Origin' (non-differential) variant scores 0.5134 (CodeLlama-7b) and 0.2902 (Llemma-7b) vs Llama-2-7B; full AttnDiff (differential) recovers 0.9890/0.9856 (delta +0.4756/+0.6954); for unrelated models (avg across 8) Origin averages 0.3731 vs Diff averages 0.1647 (delta -0.2084), e.g. Llama3-8B: 0.4751 -> 0.2299
Headline separation claim: similarity > 0.98 for related/derivative models vs < 0.22 for unrelated architectures, using M=60 probes
Probe-count ablation (Fig. 2): M<=15 probes gives high spurious similarity among unrelated models; M=60 chosen as the efficiency/reliability trade-off point
baselines_compared: PCS and ICS (Zeng et al. 2024; hyeon Yoon et al. 2025) -- parameter-based fingerprints
Logits (Yang and Wu 2024) and REEF (Zhang et al. 2024) -- representation-based fingerprints
LLMMap (Pasquini et al. 2025, USENIX Security 2025) -- semantic/output-based fingerprint
